strategy_justification: Direct HTTP returns full server-rendered page content. No anti-bot protection detected. All product data is available in JSON-LD (with non-standard capitalized keys), OG meta tags, inline serverSideEvents JavaScript, and static HTML DOM. No JavaScript rendering needed. This strategy worked for 3/5 products in the previous attempt; the 2 failures were discontinued products that redirect to homepage, not connectivity issues.
proxy_justification: Direct HTTP connection works perfectly without any proxy. No blocking or rate limiting observed. Previous test showed 3/5 products fetched successfully; the 2 failures were server-side redirects to homepage (discontinued products), not proxy/blocking issues.
jsonld_parsing_notes: CRITICAL: The Product JSON-LD uses NON-STANDARD CAPITALIZED property names. Code MUST access: Product.Name (not name), Product.Mpn (not mpn), Product.Description (not description), Product.Image (not image), Product.Brand.Name (not brand.name), Product.Offers.Price (not offers.price), Product.Offers.PriceCurrency (not offers.priceCurrency), Product.Offers.Availability (not offers.availability), Product.AggregateRating.RatingValue (not aggregateRating.ratingValue). The JSON-LD raw content may have concatenated JSON after the main object — parse by finding first '{' to last '}' brace pair. There are exactly 2 JSON-LD blocks: BreadcrumbList (index 0) and Product (index 1).

server_side_events.access_method: Regex parse from inline script: var serverSideEvents = [{...}];
server_side_events.rating_warning: CRITICAL: serverSideEvents.dimension13 is a JavaScript float that may have floating-point precision issues (e.g., 4.099999904632568 instead of 4.1). MUST round to 1 decimal place: format(round(float(value), 1), '.1f'). Prefer JSON-LD AggregateRating.RatingValue which is already rounded (4.2), or use serverSideEvents with rounding.

promo_price_warning.explanation: This is a promotional coupon price (e.g., 'DOUBLE45' code). It is NOT the actual product price. Do NOT extract this as the product price. Extract only from JSON-LD Product.Offers.Price, .ae-normal-price .ae-price--normal (when not on sale), or .ae-sale-price .ae-price--now (when on sale).
promo_price_warning.original_price_logic: When .ae-sale-price does NOT have class 'hide', the product is on sale: .ae-sale-price .ae-price--was = original price, .ae-sale-price .ae-price--now = sale price. When .ae-sale-price HAS class 'hide' (current state for this product), use .ae-normal-price .ae-price--normal as the price (which equals the original price since there's no sale).

page_validation.description: MUST validate that the fetched page is actually a product page before extracting data. Some product URLs redirect to the homepage (200 OK but homepage content). Without validation, homepage data gets extracted as product data.

checks.description: Most reliable check. Homepage will NOT have a Product JSON-LD block.

checks.check: Match 'pageType: "Product"' in inline phe JavaScript config
checks.description: phe.page.pageType is set to 'Product' on product pages, 'Home' on homepage.
Product canonical URLs always end in .aspx. Homepage canonical is just the domain.
OG type meta tag is 'product' on product pages, may differ on other pages.

page_validation.on_failure_action: If page validation fails, set remarks to 'Received non-product page (possibly discontinued/redirected). URL may be invalid or product removed.' Do NOT overwrite product.url from the canonical link. Keep the original src_url as the url.

title.note: JSON-LD uses 'Name' (capital N). OG title includes site suffix that should be stripped if used as fallback.

price.note: JSON-LD returns numeric 49.99. CSS returns '$49.99' string. OG returns '49.99' string. JSON-LD is cleanest source. NOTE: There is a promo price '$27.49' in .jcpoffer-pricerange that MUST be ignored.

original_price.note: Only available when .ae-sale-price does NOT have class 'hide'. For this product, sale price div is hidden (class 'hide'), meaning no sale is active, so original_price equals price.
original_price.fix_required: CRITICAL FIX: After extracting text from .ae-price--was with .get_text(strip=True), the text may contain extraneous promotional text like '89.99Excluded from promotion.Save 58.49 (65%)'. MUST apply regex to extract ONLY the price number: re.search(r'[\d.]+', was_text).group(0). Then format with format_price(). Do NOT use the raw text directly. Also consider: og:price:standard_amount meta tag may exist on sale products (not verified on this product since it's not on sale).

availability.note: JSON-LD returns full URL 'http://schema.org/InStock'. OG returns short form 'instock'. Normalize to standard values like 'InStock'.

description.test_result: While scandalously exposed breasts do what they're best at, you'll cast a mesmerizing spell in hypnotic swirl patterned mesh! This babydoll's soft & stretchy, with satin bow detail and matching crotchless thong. Nylon & spandex. Imported.
description.note: JSON-LD has the clean full description. DOM version is split across multiple accordion sections. Prefer JSON-LD.

sku.note: JSON-LD uses 'Mpn' (capital M) for the SKU field.

image_url.note: JSON-LD provides 250x250 version. OG provides 0x0 (full resolution). Prefer OG for higher quality.

images.note: VERIFIED via browser: 11 main images + 11 thumbnails = 22 total. Main images are in .glider-track > .glider-slide > img. The previous scraper used '.ae-product-carousel .glider-slide img' which should work. If images are empty, try the more specific selector '.ae-product-carousel .glider-track > .glider-slide img' to avoid thumbnail duplicates. Deduplicate by URL. Filter out any data: URI sources. IMPORTANT: The .ae-product-carousel__thumbs section also has .glider-slide img elements — to get unique gallery images only, prefer '.ae-main-product-carousel .glider-track .glider-slide img' or deduplicate by URL.

rating.note: JSON-LD truncates to 4.2. serverSideEvents has more precise 4.25. CRITICAL: serverSideEvents.dimension13 can have floating-point precision errors (e.g., 4.099999904632568). MUST round: format(round(float(value), 1), '.1f').
rating.fix_required: MUST round rating value to 1 decimal place. If using serverSideEvents, apply rounding. If using JSON-LD, it's already rounded but less precise.

breadcrumbs.note: BreadcrumbList JSON-LD is available. CSS fallback uses '.breadcrumb a, .breadcrumb span'.

variants.note: First option is always 'Please make a selection' with empty value — skip it. Format: {Color}/{Size} (${Price}). Color and size are parsed from the text.

color_variants.note: All possible colors from serverSideEvents. The select dropdown only shows currently available combos.

size_variants.note: Empty for this product. Sizes must be parsed from variant option text (e.g., 'Small/Med', 'Queen', 'Large/Xlrg').

url.fix_required: CRITICAL FIX: Do NOT overwrite product.url from canonical if the canonical does not look like a product URL. If canonical does NOT contain '.aspx' or points to the homepage, keep the original input URL as product.url. This prevents homepage URL replacing the product URL for discontinued/redirected products.

recommended_extraction_priority.rationale: JSON-LD has the richest product data with all core fields. OG meta tags are a reliable fallback for price/currency/brand/availability. CSS selectors work for title, price, images, variants, and breadcrumbs. serverSideEvents provides additional data like colors array, precise rating (with rounding), stock status, and category path.

retry_adjustments.proxy_escalation_reason: No proxy escalation needed. Previous failures were due to code bugs (missing page validation, text cleanup, float rounding), not connectivity issues. 3/5 products fetched successfully with direct HTTP.

changes_from_previous.change: Selector verified as working. Changed recommended selector to more specific '.ae-product-carousel .glider-track > .glider-slide img' to get only main carousel images (avoiding thumbnail duplicates). Added deduplication by URL.
changes_from_previous.reason: Previous test reported images empty for all products, but verification confirms the selector works. May have been a transient parsing issue or the code was filtering them out incorrectly.

changes_from_previous.change: Added fix: apply regex re.search(r'[\d.]+', was_text).group(0) to extract only the numeric price from .ae-price--was text, which may contain extraneous promotional text.
changes_from_previous.reason: Product 1 original_price was '89.99Excluded from promotion.Save 58.49 (65%)' instead of '$89.99'.

changes_from_previous.change: NEW: Added page type validation checks. After fetch, verify page is a product page by checking: (1) Product JSON-LD exists, (2) phe.page.pageType is 'Product', (3) canonical URL contains '.aspx'. If validation fails, set remarks and skip extraction.
changes_from_previous.reason: Products 3 and 4 returned homepage HTML (200 OK) instead of product page. Scraper extracted homepage data as product data.

changes_from_previous.change: Do NOT overwrite product.url from canonical if canonical does not contain '.aspx' or points to homepage. Keep original input URL.
changes_from_previous.reason: Products 3 and 4 had url overwritten with homepage URL from canonical link.

changes_from_previous.change: Round serverSideEvents.dimension13 to 1 decimal place using format(round(float(value), 1), '.1f').
changes_from_previous.reason: Product 2 rating was raw float '4.099999904632568' instead of '4.1'.
